title: Children
wikitext:
[]: Children?             Quote Post by Sventevith » Thu Feb 24, 2005 5:57 pm Why is it "First Children" and so on? This might be a stupid question, couldn't find any on the site or if I searched the forums - and I can recall I've read something about it sometime... but I got a brainfart and now it's completely ereased from my mind! Could you help me out?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Feb 24, 2005 6:04 pm Translation from Japanese literly its first children.

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Thu Feb 24, 2005 6:15 pm DatDude wrote: Translation from Japanese literly its first children.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Feb 24, 2005 6:20 pm Umm I thing either matt greefield or tiffany grant mentioned it on the ADV commentary track so yes I think its true. Not that every thing they say their is its just somthing simple . on a side note I love your avatar :)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 24, 2005 6:28 pm There's a little discussion about the term at cut 184 here .

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Thu Feb 24, 2005 6:30 pm DatDude wrote: Umm I thing either matt greefield or tiffany grant mentioned it on the ADV commentary track so yes I think its true. Not that every thing they say their is its just somthing simple . on a side note I love your avatar :)

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Thu Feb 24, 2005 7:07 pm Reichu wrote: There's a little discussion about the term at cut 184 here .

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Thu Feb 24, 2005 7:10 pm If we're going to be funny and non-serious... First Children fits on Rei, huh? ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Thu Feb 24, 2005 7:12 pm Oh please, it's nothing but a little bit of Engrish. Really, what meaning do you lose by translating Children into Child? The fact that they are one among others? We are kind of told that fact pretty early on.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Feb 24, 2005 11:09 pm Rei was the first designated pilot, and I think the idea is that she's called the First Children cuz there's a whole gaggle of spare Reis in a tank down in NERV's basement. Apparently the convention of using the plural "Children" was continued with the others (Asuka = "Second Children", etc) for sake of consistency. MDWigs is a member of this forum, so perhaps he can confirm (or not) if that is what Kaji was getting at...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Feb 24, 2005 11:37 pm I was always curious to know if the term "children" was some sort of Japanese minded flub. Correct me if I'm wrong, but the Japanese don't ordinarily have plurilizations of nouns, do they?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 24, 2005 11:44 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I was always curious to know if the term "children" was some sort of Japanese minded flub. Correct me if I'm wrong, but the Japanese don't ordinarily have plurilizations of nouns, do they?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon May 23, 2005 8:40 pm Perhaps the word "Children" was chosen over "child" to emphasise how important these pilots are. Our children are our future, in whom we place our hopes and dreams, and to whom we will entrust our legacy. The Children who are the pilots have on their shoulders the weight of the hope of all of humanity. Every time they sortie, the prayers of everyone on earth follow them out. They are no longer simply a child. They are "children". The hope of the present, for the fate of the future? Thoughts anyone?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon May 23, 2005 9:14 pm I still say they came up with their "explanation" to cover their dopey gramatical error, just as the came up with the whole "manifesting the plug suit" business to cover up their continuity error in ep. 20.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 23, 2005 9:39 pm So they made Rei one of many solely so that she could be "First ChildREN", thus "explaining" this "error"? I don't think they would have let it bother them that much. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed May 25, 2005 6:34 am I remember reading that foreign loan-words are often absorbed into Japanese in their plural form - the example that sticks in my mind is that of grapefruit - so using Children rather than Child would just be linguistic SOP, and not anything by way of subtle hint at the Reiquarium.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed May 25, 2005 8:52 am Mr. Tines wrote: I remember reading that foreign loan-words are often absorbed into Japanese in their plural form - the example that sticks in my mind is that of grapefruit - so using Children rather than Child would just be linguistic SOP, and not anything by way of subtle hint at the Reiquarium.

[]:             Quote Post by Shwiggie » Fri May 27, 2005 1:44 pm Wasn't Shinji's whole class candidates for use in Project E under the designation of "Fourth Children"? Rei, Asuka, and Shinji weren't picked randomly out of a pool, but neither was Kaworu, and he came after the seemingly randomly chosen Toji. Rather than calling Rei, for example, "First Child", would it not make some sort of sense to say "First Children" as the explicit designation that she's the first of all "The Children" to be used in the program? Not saying it isn't just a case of Engrish, but there's a method to a whole lot more of the Evangelion madness...why not assume there's a method to this one, as well?
summary:
[]: Children?             Quote Post by Sventevith » Thu Feb 24, 2005 5:57 pm Why is it "First Children" and so on? This might be a stupid question, couldn't find any on the site or if I searched the forums - and I can recall I've read something about it sometime... but I got a brainfart and now it's completely ereased from my mind! Could you help me out?

[]:           &nbs
